question:
Assume that the product failures from an assembly line are 5%.
Suppose that if a product that is defective, a diagnostic test indicates that it is defective 92% of the time, and if the product is good, the test indicates that it is good
96% of the time. Calculate the probability that the product is good given that the test
indicates defective?
answer:
Let
A = product is good (not defective)
$\mathrm{A}_{\mathrm{c}}=$ product is defective
B = a test indicates that it is good (not defective)
$\mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{c}}=\mathrm{a}$ test indicates it is defective
Then
$$
P(A)=0.95, P\left(A_c\right)=0.05 ; P(B \mid A)=0.96, P\left(B_c \mid A_c\right)=0.92
$$


The probability that the product is good given that the test indicates defective is:

$$
\begin{aligned}
P\left(A \mid B_c\right) & =\frac{P\left(A \cap B_c\right)}{P\left(B_c\right)} \\
& =\frac{P\left(B_c \mid A\right) P(A)}{P\left(B_c \mid A\right) P(A)+P\left(B_c \mid A_c\right) P\left(A_c\right)} \\
& =\frac{0.04 * 0.95}{(0.04 * 0.95)+(0.92 * 0.05)}=0.4524
\end{aligned}
$$
